channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Social Security cost-of-living benefits increase announcement coming Thursday
item.description: Tens of millions of Social Security recipients will learn Thursday how big a cost-of-living boost they’ll get to their benefits next year.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A federal judge will hear more evidence on whether to reopen voter registration in Georgia
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — At least for now, a federal judge won’t order the state of Georgia to reopen voter registration for November’s elections. U.S.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: This is FEMA's role in preparing for Hurricane Milton
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Hurricane Milton is set to slam Florida on Wednesday evening — the second hurricane to strike in two weeks — and the federal agency tasked with responding to disasters is once again positioning supplies and personnel where they're needed and coordinating with state and local offici
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mayor Frey vetoes carbon emission fee adopted by city council
item.description: In his veto letter Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey said he agrees with the aims of the fee on polluters, but he said the council’s action amounts to an “unauthorized tax.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Trump campaigns in 'make or break' Pennsylvania
item.description: Former President Trump barnstormed Pennsylvania on Wednesday as he and his campaign have made clear the commonwealth could make or break his chances of winning in November. Trump held rallies in President Biden’s hometown of Scranton and in Reading.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Ohio Lt. Gov. encourages support for Trump’s ‘swamp the vote’ strategy as key state's early voting opens
item.description: Early voting began Tuesday in Ohio, where Fox News Digital spoke to the state's Republican Lt. Gov. Jon Husted about the GOP push to turn out the early vote.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Over 5,700 children in 5-year period had gender surgeries, most from 5 liberal states: watchdog
item.description: A national database reveals more than 5,700 minors underwent gender reassignment surgeries or treatments from 2019-2023, totaling nearly $120 million in charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florida has nearly all ballots counted on Election Day, while California can take weeks. This is why
item.description: Why is it that some states have nearly all the ballots counted within hours of polls closing while others take weeks?
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump secretly sent Putin COVID-19 tests during pandemic shortage, a new book reports
item.description: Vice President Harris is criticizing Trump over the reported shipment, which is detailed in a new book by the journalist Bob Woodward. The president's campaign has denied the reporting.
item.dc:creator: Jason Breslow, MPR News

item.title: Harris, Walz appeared on ABC's 'The View' and 'Jimmy Kimmel Live'
item.description: The Democratic presidential ticket is on a media blitz in New York. Vice President Kamala Harris appeared Tuesday on ABC's “The View," and her running mate, Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz appeared on ABC's “Jimmy Kimmel Live” on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Cook Political Report shifts Wisconsin Senate race toward GOP
item.description: The Cook Political Report on Tuesday shifted the Wisconsin Senate race from "lean Democrat" to "toss up," handing Republican Eric Hovde a boost as he tries to unseat Sen. Tammy Baldwin (D-Wis.).
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, Harris look to score points amid Helene response
item.description: Once considered a political-free zone, disaster relief has become fair game for campaign point scoring on both sides of the aisle.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Dolton Mayor Tiffany Henyard walks out as trustees address budget deficit
item.description: "I just want to come here and lay the law down as it relates to what's going on in our village, where we are, and where we're going," Dolton Mayor Tiffany Henyard said.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, NewsNation

item.title: DeSantis pushes back on 'delusional' Harris remark about Hurricane Milton calls
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) said Monday that it was “delusional” for Vice President Kamala Harris to accuse him of “political gamesmanship” after reports emerged that he did not take the Democratic presidential nominee’s calls as his state braces for Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Business groups push back after Walz says they supported paid leave program
item.description: While some small business owners advocated for a government-managed leave proposal, the state’s largest business groups sought to block the proposal in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Harris reveals owning a Glock: 'Of course' I've fired it
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris said in a "60 Minutes" interview that aired Monday that she owns a Glock and has fired it at a shooting range in another example of how she and her campaign have made a point to highlight her gun ownership.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane Milton may be October surprise in presidential battle
item.description: Strategists in both parties are looking back to Hurricane Sandy, which slammed into the mid-Atlantic states shortly before Election Day 2012.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Poll: In the midst of a tight presidential race, Trump draws support from voters disillusioned with democracy
item.description: New polling shows this year’s Harris v. Trump presidential race is too close to call, with Trump drawing support from voters who are disillusioned with democracy. Results from the McCourtney Institute for Democracy’s Sept. 23-27, 2024 Mood of the Nation Poll.
item.dc:creator: Eric Plutzer and Craig Helmstetter, MPR News

item.title: October surprise 'huge myth,' Harris will win: Allan Lichtman
item.description: The "October surprise" is a "huge myth" in American politics, according to the historian who devised the “Keys to the White House” formula.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump intends to use military to round up opponents: Michael Cohen
item.description: “The big warning that I want people to understand is when Donald Trump says something, stop sane washing it," Michael Cohen said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: It’s a close race for Michigan. These factors could decide it for Harris or Trump
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris needs to win back Michigan voters President Joe Biden looked poised to lose. Donald Trump’s appeal remains strong here, but he’s not without his own struggles.
item.dc:creator: Majd Al-Waheidi, MPR News

item.title: Trump urges his supporters to deliver victory in his return to scene of first assassination attempt
item.description: BUTLER, Pa. (AP) — Donald Trump returned on Saturday to the Pennsylvania fairgrounds where he was nearly assassinated in July, urging a large crowd to deliver an Election Day victory that he tied to his survival of the shooting.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, JILL COLVIN and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Voters in 13 states have less than a week to register. When is the deadline in Illinois?
item.description: The 2024 presidential election is fast approaching, and in some states, the voter registration deadline is as soon as this week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, WGN 9

item.title: Walz on Tiananmen Square screw-up: 'I'm a knucklehead at times'
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), pressed at the vice presidential debate over false statements he made in which he said he was present during the Tiananmen Square protests in 1989, said: “I’m a knucklehead at times.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, Fox 5
